response: The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which came into effect in May 2018, significantly altered how organizations manage personal data, applying to any company processing data of EU residents. Key provisions include the right to data access, erasure, and portability, and the requirement for explicit consent. Non-compliance can result in fines up to 4% of global annual revenue or 20 million euros, with major tech companies like Meta, Amazon, and Google facing substantial penalties. While GDPR has inspired similar laws globally and established data privacy as a fundamental right, it has also been criticized for creating compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement.
